neighborhoods.name: Ajijic Centro
neighborhoods.description: Cobblestone village center with art galleries, restaurants, and Mexico's largest foreign resident community
neighborhoods.character: Artistic village, huge expat community, walkable

housing.predominantType: Colonial houses, apartments, and converted properties

neighborhoods.character_notes: The historic and commercial heart of Ajijic. Most walkable area with best access to cultural attractions, restaurants, and public services.

neighborhoods.name: Chapala Town
neighborhoods.description: Larger lakeside town with more Mexican character, municipal services, and the lakefront malecon
neighborhoods.character: Mexican lakeside town, malecon, local services

housing.predominantType: Houses and apartments at lower Ajijic prices

neighborhoods.character_notes: Established residential area offering a quieter lifestyle while maintaining reasonable access to Ajijic's center and services.

neighborhoods.name: Jocotepec
neighborhoods.description: Western lake town with indigenous heritage, lower costs, and growing expat interest
neighborhoods.character_notes: More affordable option for budget-conscious retirees. May require a car or longer transit times to reach central amenities.

neighborhoods.name: La Floresta / Upper Ajijic
neighborhoods.description: Hillside area above Ajijic center with panoramic lake views and established expat community
neighborhoods.character: Hillside expat, lake views, gated communities

housing.predominantType: Modern houses, gated communities, and custom homes

neighborhoods.character_notes: Area with established international community and expat-oriented services. English is more commonly spoken here than in other neighborhoods.
